Question: Put the equation $$\frac{( x + 3 ) y \prime}{2 x - 3 y - 4} = 5$$ into standard form and identify $$p ( x )$$ and $$q ( x ) .$$
Find the general solution to the differential equation $$( x - 2 ) y \prime + y = 3 x^{2} + 2 x .$$ Assume $$x > 2 .$$
Solve the initial-value problem $$y \prime - 2 y = 4 x + 3 y ( 0 ) = -2 .$$

The weight of a penny is $$2.5$$ grams (United States Mint, “Coin Specifications,” accessed April 9, 2015, http://www.usmint.gov/about_the_mint/?action=coin_specifications), and the upper observation deck of the Empire State Building is $$369$$ meters above the street. Since the penny is a small and relatively smooth object, air resistance acting on the penny is actually quite small. We assume the air resistance is numerically equal to $$0.0025 v .$$ Furthermore, the penny is dropped with no initial velocity imparted to it. Set up an initial-value problem that represents the falling penny. Solve the problem for $$v ( t ) .$$ What is the terminal velocity of the penny (i.e., calculate the limit of the velocity as $$t$$ approaches infinity)?
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}\frac{d v}{d t} & = & \text{unicode[:-]} v - 9.8 \\ v ( 0 ) & = & 0\end{matrix}\right .$$ $$v ( t ) = 9.8 ( e^{\text{unicode[:-]} t} - 1 )$$ $$\underset{t \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} v \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \underset{t \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 9.8 \left(\right. e^{- t} - 1 \left.\right) \left.\right) = -9.8 \text{m}/\text{s} \approx - 21.922 \text{mph}$$

Question: A circuit has in series an electromotive force given by $$E = 20 \text{sin} 5 t$$ V, a capacitor with capacitance $$0.02 \text{F} ,$$ and a resistor of $$8 \text{omega} .$$ If the initial charge is $$4 \text{C} ,$$ find the charge at time $$t > 0 .$$
Answer: Initial-value problem: $$8 q^{\prime} + \frac{1}{0.02} q = 20 \text{sin} 5 t , q ( 0 ) = 4$$ $$q ( t ) = \frac{10 \text{sin} 5 t - 8 \text{cos} 5 t + 172 e^{-6.25 t}}{41}$$
